--- title: "Unit 4: Statistics" number-sections: true number-depth: 2 execute: echo: false --- ## 🎯 Learning Targets By the end of this unit, you should be able to: **LT1 — Averages and Range from a List** - Calculate the **mean**, **median**, **mode** and **range** from a list of values - Find a **missing value** given the mean of a dataset - Find the **new mean** when a value is added to a dataset - Find two unknown values given their **ratio** and the overall mean - Read and interpret a **stem and leaf diagram** to find averages and range **LT2 — Frequency Tables (Ungrouped)** - Calculate the **mean** from a frequency table using $\bar{x} = \dfrac{\sum fx}{\sum f}$ - Find the **median** from a frequency table using cumulative frequencies - Identify the **mode** from a frequency table - Find the largest and smallest possible value of an unknown frequency $x$ given a target **median** **LT3 — Grouped Frequency Tables** - Identify the **modal class** from a grouped frequency table - Identify the **median class** from a grouped frequency table - **Estimate the mean** using midpoints: $\bar{x} \approx \dfrac{\sum fx}{\sum f}$ - Calculate a **combined mean** when two groups with different sizes are merged - Find **missing frequencies** $a$ and $b$ given total frequency and estimated mean **LT4 — Cumulative Frequency** - Build a **cumulative frequency table** from a frequency table - Read values from a cumulative frequency table (how many below / above a value) - Use a **cumulative frequency graph** to estimate $Q_1$, $Q_2$ (median) and $Q_3$ - Estimate the **IQR** from a cumulative frequency graph - Estimate the number of items **above a given value** - Find missing values **A** and **B** in a cumulative frequency table **LT5 — Box Plots and IQR** - Calculate the **IQR** from raw data: $\text{IQR} = Q_3 - Q_1$ - Draw a **box-and-whisker plot** from a five-number summary - Identify **outliers** using the rule: value $> Q_3 + 1.5 \times \text{IQR}$ or $< Q_1 - 1.5 \times \text{IQR}$ - Find unknown values in an ordered list given conditions on median, mode and range --- ## 🗂️ Formula Cards :::::::::::: formula-card ### Statistics — Key Formulas & Rules {.unnumbered} #### Averages and Range {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-yellow **💡 Pro Tip** Always **sort your data first** before finding the median or quartiles. ::: ::: rule-group **Mean**: $$\bar{x} = \frac{\sum x}{n} = \frac{\text{sum of all values}}{\text{number of values}}$$ **Median**: Middle value when data is ordered.\ If $n$ is even: mean of the two middle values. **Mode**: Most frequently occurring value. **Range**: $$\text{Range} = \text{maximum} - \text{minimum}$$ **Example**: Data: $3, 7, 7, 9, 14$ $$\bar{x} = \frac{3+7+7+9+14}{5} = 8 \qquad \text{Median} = 7 \qquad \text{Mode} = 7 \qquad \text{Range} = 11$$ ::: #### Mean from a Frequency Table {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-blue **🎯 Memory Trick** Multiply each value by its frequency first — then divide by the **total frequency**, not the number of rows! ::: ::: rule-group $$\bar{x} = \frac{\sum fx}{\sum f}$$ | $x$ | $f$ | $fx$ | |:---:|:---:|:---:| | 2 | 3 | 6 | | 4 | 7 | 28 | | 6 | 5 | 30 | | **Total** | **15** | **64** | $$\bar{x} = \frac{64}{15} \approx 4.3$$ ::: #### Grouped Frequency Tables {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Mistake** Use the **midpoint** of each class — not the lower or upper boundary! ::: ::: rule-group **Estimated mean**: $$\bar{x} \approx \frac{\sum fx}{\sum f} \quad \text{where } x = \text{midpoint of class}$$ **Modal class**: Class with the **highest frequency**. **Median class**: Class containing the $\dfrac{n}{2}$th value (use cumulative frequency). **Example**: Class $10 \leq x < 20$, frequency $= 8$ → midpoint $x = 15$, so $fx = 120$ ::: #### Cumulative Frequency {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-orange **📝 Quick Method** Read across from the CF axis to the curve, then **down** to the $x$-axis. ::: ::: rule-group **Building CF table**: Each row = previous cumulative total $+$ current frequency. **Reading quartiles from a CF graph** (total = $n$): | Measure | Read at CF value | |:--------|:----------------:| | Lower quartile $Q_1$ | $\dfrac{n}{4}$ | | Median $Q_2$ | $\dfrac{n}{2}$ | | Upper quartile $Q_3$ | $\dfrac{3n}{4}$ | $$\text{IQR} = Q_3 - Q_1$$ ::: #### Box Plots and Outliers {.unnumbered} ::: rule-group **Five-number summary**: Min, $Q_1$, Median, $Q_3$, Max **Box plot**: Box spans $Q_1$ to $Q_3$. Median line inside. Whiskers extend to min and max. **Outlier rule**: $$\text{Outlier if: } x > Q_3 + 1.5 \times \text{IQR} \quad \text{or} \quad x < Q_1 - 1.5 \times \text{IQR}$$ **Example**: $Q_1 = 35,\ Q_3 = 55,\ \text{IQR} = 20$\ Upper fence $= 55 + 1.5 \times 20 = 85$\ Any value $> 85$ is an outlier. ::: #### Combined Mean {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-yellow **💡 Pro Tip** You **cannot** just average two means — you must use the totals! ::: ::: rule-group $$\bar{x}_{\text{combined}} = \frac{n_1 \bar{x}_1 + n_2 \bar{x}_2}{n_1 + n_2}$$ **Example**: Group 1: $n=100$, mean $= 18.2$. Group 2: $n=50$, mean $= 17.3$ $$\bar{x} = \frac{100 \times 18.2 + 50 \times 17.3}{150} = \frac{1820 + 865}{150} = \frac{2685}{150} = 17.9$$ ::: :::::::::::: --- ## 📊 Part A — Averages and Range {#sec-averages} ::: {.callout-note icon=false} ## 🔑 Key Formulas $$\bar{x} = \frac{\sum x}{n} \qquad \text{Median} = \text{middle value (sorted)} \qquad \text{Range} = \text{max} - \text{min}$$ ::: :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-avg-1} ### Mean from a List Calculate the mean. a) $$15,\ 17,\ 20,\ 2,\ 8$$ b) $$17,\ 10,\ 7,\ 3,\ 18,\ 17,\ 12,\ 4$$ c) $$13,\ 3,\ 15,\ 6,\ 13,\ 14$$ d) $$11,\ 10,\ 16,\ 7,\ 11,\ 13,\ 6,\ 16$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\bar{x} = 12.4$$ $$\quad \dfrac{15+17+20+2+8}{5} = 12.4$$ **b)** $$\bar{x} = 11$$ $$\quad \dfrac{17+10+7+3+18+17+12+4}{8} = 11$$ **c)** $$\bar{x} = 10.67$$ $$\quad \dfrac{13+3+15+6+13+14}{6} = 10.67$$ **d)** $$\bar{x} = 11.25$$ $$\quad \dfrac{11+10+16+7+11+13+6+16}{8} = 11.25$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Sum of values Division Mean formula
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-avg-2} ### Median from a List Find the median. a) $$1,\ 2,\ 13,\ 15,\ 19,\ 20$$ b) $$5,\ 7,\ 8,\ 10,\ 12,\ 18$$ c) $$3,\ 11,\ 14,\ 15,\ 16,\ 18,\ 19$$ d) $$2,\ 2,\ 6,\ 8,\ 8,\ 10,\ 14,\ 16,\ 20$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Median} = 14$$ $$\quad \text{Mean of positions 3 and 4: }\frac{13+15}{2}=14$$ **b)** $$\text{Median} = 9$$ $$\quad \text{Mean of positions 3 and 4: }\frac{8+10}{2}=9$$ **c)** $$\text{Median} = 15$$ $$\quad \text{Ordered. Position }\frac{n+1}{2}=4\text{th value} = 15$$ **d)** $$\text{Median} = 8$$ $$\quad \text{Ordered. Position }\frac{n+1}{2}=5\text{th value} = 8$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Ordering data Locating middle
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-avg-3} ### Mode from a List Find the mode. a) $$2,\ 11,\ 2,\ 9,\ 2,\ 2$$ b) $$2,\ 2,\ 2,\ 2,\ 3,\ 8$$ c) $$5,\ 5,\ 5,\ 10,\ 3,\ 11$$ d) $$11,\ 3,\ 11,\ 3,\ 3,\ 3$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Mode} = 2$$ $$\quad \text{Most frequent value(s): }2$$ **b)** $$\text{Mode} = 2$$ $$\quad \text{Most frequent value(s): }2$$ **c)** $$\text{Mode} = 5$$ $$\quad \text{Most frequent value(s): }5$$ **d)** $$\text{Mode} = 3$$ $$\quad \text{Most frequent value(s): }3$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Frequency counting
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-avg-4} ### Range from a List Find the range. a) $$24,\ 3,\ 12,\ 30,\ 12,\ 9,\ 4,\ 10,\ 16$$ b) $$20,\ 7,\ 14,\ 29,\ 17,\ 23,\ 30,\ 9$$ c) $$10,\ 18,\ 17,\ 21,\ 17,\ 4,\ 7,\ 17,\ 23$$ d) $$30,\ 14,\ 6,\ 15,\ 19,\ 20,\ 13,\ 19$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Range} = 27$$ $$\quad 30 - 3 = 27$$ **b)** $$\text{Range} = 23$$ $$\quad 30 - 7 = 23$$ **c)** $$\text{Range} = 19$$ $$\quad 23 - 4 = 19$$ **d)** $$\text{Range} = 24$$ $$\quad 30 - 6 = 24$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Max min Subtraction
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🟡 Medium ::::: {#exr-avg-5} ### Mean, Median, Mode and Range Find the mean, median, mode and range. a) $$4,\ 8,\ 13,\ 3,\ 8,\ 9,\ 10,\ 8,\ 9$$ b) $$3,\ 9,\ 15,\ 14,\ 15,\ 15,\ 11$$ c) $$13,\ 12,\ 7,\ 10,\ 10,\ 7,\ 7,\ 8,\ 4$$ d) $$5,\ 15,\ 6,\ 15,\ 8,\ 8,\ 13,\ 8$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Mean}=8,\ \text{Median}=8,\ \text{Mode}=8,\ \text{Range}=10$$ $$\quad \bar{x}=\frac{72}{9}=8\quad \text{Median}=8\quad \text{Mode}=8\quad \text{Range}=10$$ **b)** $$\text{Mean}=11.71,\ \text{Median}=14,\ \text{Mode}=15,\ \text{Range}=12$$ $$\quad \bar{x}=\frac{82}{7}=11.71\quad \text{Median}=14\quad \text{Mode}=15\quad \text{Range}=12$$ **c)** $$\text{Mean}=8.67,\ \text{Median}=8,\ \text{Mode}=7,\ \text{Range}=9$$ $$\quad \bar{x}=\frac{78}{9}=8.67\quad \text{Median}=8\quad \text{Mode}=7\quad \text{Range}=9$$ **d)** $$\text{Mean}=9.75,\ \text{Median}=8,\ \text{Mode}=8,\ \text{Range}=10$$ $$\quad \bar{x}=\frac{78}{8}=9.75\quad \text{Median}=8\quad \text{Mode}=8\quad \text{Range}=10$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
All averages Ordering data
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🔴 Challenging ::::: {#exr-avg-6} ### Find Missing Value / Updated Mean Find the missing value or new mean. a) $$\text{Mean} = 14,\quad n=6\\[4pt]\text{Numbers: }14,\ 8,\ 13,\ 7,\ 4,\ x$$ b) $$\text{Mean} = 26,\quad\text{three values: }30,\ 35,\ 30\\[4pt]\text{Other two in ratio }1:4$$ c) $$\text{Mean} = 11,\quad n=5\\[4pt]\text{Numbers: }15,\ 14,\ 8,\ 4,\ x$$ d) $$\text{Mean of }6\text{ numbers} = 3,\quad\text{new value} = 12$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$x = 38$$ $$\quad \text{Total} = 14\times6=84\quad x = 84-(14+8+13+7+4) = 38$$ **b)** $$7\text{ and }28$$ $$\quad \text{Step 1: Total}=26\times5=130\\[4pt]\text{Step 2: Remaining}=130-(30+35+30)=35\\[4pt]\text{Step 3: Ratio }1:4\text{ → }5\text{ equal parts, each }=\frac{35}{5}=7\\[4pt]\text{Step 4: Numbers are }7\text{ and }28$$ **c)** $$x = 14$$ $$\quad \text{Total} = 11\times5=55\quad x = 55-(15+14+8+4) = 14$$ **d)** $$\text{New mean} = 4.29$$ $$\quad \text{Old total} = 3\times6 = 18\quad \text{New total} = 18+12 = 30\quad \bar{x}_{\text{new}} = \frac{30}{7} = 4.29$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Rearranging formula Sum from mean
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-avg-1} ### Averages from Stem & Leaf Use the stem and leaf diagram to find the median, range and modal class. ```{=html}
``` a) $\text{Find the median.}$ b) $\text{Find the range.}$ c) $\text{Find the mode.}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $\text{Median} = 31$ $$\quad \text{Ordered: }27\text{ values, middle} = 31$$ **b)** $\text{Range} = 47$ $$\quad 59 - 12 = 47$$ **c)** $\text{Mode} = 17$ $$\quad \text{Most frequent value: }17$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reading stem leaf Ordering data
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-avg-2} ### Averages from Stem & Leaf Use the stem and leaf diagram to find the median, range and modal class. ```{=html}
``` a) $\text{Find the median.}$ b) $\text{Find the range.}$ c) $\text{Find the mode.}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $\text{Median} = 37$ $$\quad \text{Ordered: }15\text{ values, middle} = 37$$ **b)** $\text{Range} = 35$ $$\quad 58 - 23 = 35$$ **c)** $\text{Mode} = 23$ $$\quad \text{Most frequent value: }23$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reading stem leaf Ordering data
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-avg-3} ### Averages from Stem & Leaf Use the stem and leaf diagram to find the median, range and modal class. ```{=html}
``` a) $\text{Find the median.}$ b) $\text{Find the range.}$ c) $\text{Find the mode.}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $\text{Median} = 60$ $$\quad \text{Ordered: }11\text{ values, middle} = 60$$ **b)** $\text{Range} = 36$ $$\quad 77 - 41 = 36$$ **c)** $\text{Mode} = 60$ $$\quad \text{Most frequent value: }60$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reading stem leaf Ordering data
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: :::::::::: --- ## 📋 Part B — Frequency Tables {#sec-freq} ::: {.callout-note icon=false} ## 🔑 Key Formula $$\bar{x} = \frac{\sum fx}{\sum f}$$ ::: :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-freq-1} ### Mean from Frequency Table Calculate the mean from the frequency table. a) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccccc} \hline x & 2 & 3 & 5 & 7 & 9 & 10 \\[4pt] \hline f & 3 & 1 & 7 & 7 & 2 & 2 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ b) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline x & 4 & 6 & 8 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline f & 4 & 4 & 6 & 6 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ c) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lccccc} \hline x & 1 & 2 & 4 & 7 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline f & 4 & 10 & 4 & 1 & 4 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ d) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline x & 2 & 4 & 5 & 6 \\[4pt] \hline f & 5 & 5 & 5 & 3 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\bar{x} = 5.95$$ $$\quad \frac{2\times3+3\times1+5\times7+7\times7+9\times2+10\times2}{22} = \frac{131}{22} = 5.95$$ **b)** $$\bar{x} = 7.1$$ $$\quad \frac{4\times4+6\times4+8\times6+9\times6}{20} = \frac{142}{20} = 7.1$$ **c)** $$\bar{x} = 3.43$$ $$\quad \frac{1\times4+2\times10+4\times4+7\times1+8\times4}{23} = \frac{79}{23} = 3.43$$ **d)** $$\bar{x} = 4.06$$ $$\quad \frac{2\times5+4\times5+5\times5+6\times3}{18} = \frac{73}{18} = 4.06$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Fx column Sum of frequencies
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-freq-3} ### Mode from Frequency Table Find the mode from the frequency table. a) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccccc} \hline x & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline f & 1 & 2 & 10 & 2 & 2 & 6 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ b) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lccccc} \hline x & 1 & 2 & 5 & 7 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline f & 4 & 4 & 9 & 9 & 1 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ c) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline x & 1 & 3 & 8 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline f & 3 & 7 & 5 & 2 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ d) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lccccc} \hline x & 2 & 4 & 7 & 9 & 10 \\[4pt] \hline f & 2 & 5 & 3 & 9 & 7 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Mode} = 3$$ $$\quad \text{Highest frequency is }10\text{, giving mode(s): }3$$ **b)** $$\text{Mode} = 5, 7$$ $$\quad \text{Highest frequency is }9\text{, giving mode(s): }5, 7$$ **c)** $$\text{Mode} = 3$$ $$\quad \text{Highest frequency is }7\text{, giving mode(s): }3$$ **d)** $$\text{Mode} = 9$$ $$\quad \text{Highest frequency is }9\text{, giving mode(s): }9$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reading frequency table
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🟡 Medium ::::: {#exr-freq-2} ### Median from Frequency Table Find the median from the frequency table. a) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccccc} \hline x & 1 & 2 & 5 & 6 & 7 & 10 \\[4pt] \hline f & 10 & 4 & 1 & 1 & 10 & 5 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ b) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lccccc} \hline x & 2 & 3 & 6 & 8 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline f & 5 & 9 & 7 & 3 & 3 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ c) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lccccc} \hline x & 2 & 3 & 7 & 9 & 10 \\[4pt] \hline f & 2 & 10 & 1 & 9 & 4 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ d) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lccccc} \hline x & 1 & 2 & 3 & 9 & 10 \\[4pt] \hline f & 1 & 6 & 7 & 8 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Median} = 6$$ $$\quad n=31\quad \text{Middle position }\frac{n+1}{2}=16.0\quad \text{Median}=6$$ **b)** $$\text{Median} = 3$$ $$\quad n=27\quad \text{Middle position }\frac{n+1}{2}=14.0\quad \text{Median}=3$$ **c)** $$\text{Median} = 8$$ $$\quad n=26\quad \text{Middle position }\frac{n+1}{2}=13.5\quad \text{Median}=8$$ **d)** $$\text{Median} = 9$$ $$\quad n=31\quad \text{Middle position }\frac{n+1}{2}=16.0\quad \text{Median}=9$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Cumulative frequency Locating middle
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-freq-4} ### Mean, Median & Mode from Table Find the mean, median and mode from the frequency table. ```{=html}
\[{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline x & 1 & 2 & 4 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline f & 3 & 4 & 5 & 7 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}\]
``` a) $$\text{Calculate the mean.}$$ b) $$\text{Find the median.}$$ c) $$\text{(c) Find the mode.}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\bar{x} = 4.58$$ $$\quad \bar{x}=\dfrac{\sum fx}{\sum f}=\dfrac{1\times3+2\times4+4\times5+8\times7}{19}=\dfrac{87}{19}=4.58$$ **b)** $$\text{Median} = 4$$ $$\quad n=19\quad \text{Position }\frac{n+1}{2}=10.0\quad \text{Build CF: }1: CF=3,\ 2: CF=7,\ 4: CF=12,\ 8: CF=19,\\quad \text{Median}=4$$ **c)** $$\text{Mode} = 8$$ $$\quad \text{Highest frequency = }7\text{ at }x=8$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Fx column Cumulative frequency Reading frequency table
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🔴 Challenging ::::: {#exr-freq-5} ### Find x Given Median (Frequency Table) Find the largest and smallest possible values of x such that the median is as given. a) $$\text{Median} = 6\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline \text{Score} & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline \text{Freq} & 3 & 7 & x & 5 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ b) $$\text{Median} = 6\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline \text{Score} & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline \text{Freq} & 5 & 9 & x & 6 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ c) $$\text{Median} = 6\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline \text{Score} & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline \text{Freq} & 4 & 5 & x & 4 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ d) $$\text{Median} = 6\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcccc} \hline \text{Score} & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline \text{Freq} & 5 & 5 & x & 7 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$x_{\min} = 0,\ x_{\max} = 4$$ $$\quad \text{For median = 6: cumulative freq up to 6} \geq \frac{n+1}{2}\quad \text{Largest: }3+7-5-1=4\quad \text{Smallest: }0$$ **b)** $$x_{\min} = 0,\ x_{\max} = 7$$ $$\quad \text{For median = 6: cumulative freq up to 6} \geq \frac{n+1}{2}\quad \text{Largest: }5+9-6-1=7\quad \text{Smallest: }0$$ **c)** $$x_{\min} = 0,\ x_{\max} = 4$$ $$\quad \text{For median = 6: cumulative freq up to 6} \geq \frac{n+1}{2}\quad \text{Largest: }4+5-4-1=4\quad \text{Smallest: }0$$ **d)** $$x_{\min} = 0,\ x_{\max} = 2$$ $$\quad \text{For median = 6: cumulative freq up to 6} \geq \frac{n+1}{2}\quad \text{Largest: }5+5-7-1=2\quad \text{Smallest: }0$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Cumulative frequency Inequality reasoning
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: :::::::::: --- ## 📦 Part C — Grouped Frequency Tables {#sec-grouped} ::: {.callout-note icon=false} ## 🔑 Key Points Use the **midpoint** of each class interval for estimated mean: $\bar{x} \approx \dfrac{\sum fx}{\sum f}$ **Modal class** = class with highest frequency. **Median class** = class containing the $\frac{n}{2}$th value. ::: :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-grp-1} ### Estimated Mean (Grouped) Estimate the mean from the grouped frequency table. a) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Midpoint} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 19 \leq x < 27 & 23 & 3 \\[4pt] \hline 27 \leq x < 35 & 31 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline 35 \leq x < 43 & 39 & 11 \\[4pt] \hline 43 \leq x < 51 & 47 & 4 \\[4pt] \hline 51 \leq x < 59 & 55 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 59 \leq x < 67 & 63 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ b) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Midpoint} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 14 \leq x < 18 & 16 & 17 \\[4pt] \hline 18 \leq x < 22 & 20 & 3 \\[4pt] \hline 22 \leq x < 26 & 24 & 19 \\[4pt] \hline 26 \leq x < 30 & 28 & 10 \\[4pt] \hline 30 \leq x < 34 & 32 & 3 \\[4pt] \hline 34 \leq x < 38 & 36 & 5 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ c) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Midpoint} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 29 \leq x < 37 & 33 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 37 \leq x < 45 & 41 & 14 \\[4pt] \hline 45 \leq x < 53 & 49 & 20 \\[4pt] \hline 53 \leq x < 61 & 57 & 5 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ d) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Midpoint} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 10 \leq x < 15 & 12.5 & 11 \\[4pt] \hline 15 \leq x < 20 & 17.5 & 16 \\[4pt] \hline 20 \leq x < 25 & 22.5 & 6 \\[4pt] \hline 25 \leq x < 30 & 27.5 & 11 \\[4pt] \hline 30 \leq x < 35 & 32.5 & 20 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Estimated mean} \approx 48$$ $$\quad \frac{23\times3+31\times9+39\times11+47\times4+55\times15+63\times15}{57}= \frac{2735}{57} \approx 48$$ **b)** $$\text{Estimated mean} \approx 23.6$$ $$\quad \frac{16\times17+20\times3+24\times19+28\times10+32\times3+36\times5}{57}= \frac{1344}{57} \approx 23.6$$ **c)** $$\text{Estimated mean} \approx 43.2$$ $$\quad \frac{33\times15+41\times14+49\times20+57\times5}{54}= \frac{2334}{54} \approx 43.2$$ **d)** $$\text{Estimated mean} \approx 23.5$$ $$\quad \frac{12.5\times11+17.5\times16+22.5\times6+27.5\times11+32.5\times20}{64}= \frac{1505}{64} \approx 23.5$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Midpoints Fx column

::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🟡 Medium ::::: {#exr-grp-3} ### Median Class (Grouped) Identify the median class. a) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 20 \leq x < 27 & 11 \\[4pt] \hline 27 \leq x < 34 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline 34 \leq x < 41 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline 41 \leq x < 48 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline 48 \leq x < 55 & 19 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ b) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 18 \leq x < 28 & 10 \\[4pt] \hline 28 \leq x < 38 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline 38 \leq x < 48 & 18 \\[4pt] \hline 48 \leq x < 58 & 14 \\[4pt] \hline 58 \leq x < 68 & 16 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ c) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 14 \leq x < 23 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 23 \leq x < 32 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 32 \leq x < 41 & 12 \\[4pt] \hline 41 \leq x < 50 & 18 \\[4pt] \hline 50 \leq x < 59 & 14 \\[4pt] \hline 59 \leq x < 68 & 12 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ d) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 9 \leq x < 19 & 8 \\[4pt] \hline 19 \leq x < 29 & 12 \\[4pt] \hline 29 \leq x < 39 & 19 \\[4pt] \hline 39 \leq x < 49 & 13 \\[4pt] \hline 49 \leq x < 59 & 3 \\[4pt] \hline 59 \leq x < 69 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Median class: }34 \leq x < 41$$ $$\quad n=54\quad \frac{n}{2}=27\quad \text{Median class: }34 \leq x < 41$$ **b)** $$\text{Median class: }38 \leq x < 48$$ $$\quad n=67\quad \frac{n}{2}=33.5\quad \text{Median class: }38 \leq x < 48$$ **c)** $$\text{Median class: }41 \leq x < 50$$ $$\quad n=86\quad \frac{n}{2}=43\quad \text{Median class: }41 \leq x < 50$$ **d)** $$\text{Median class: }29 \leq x < 39$$ $$\quad n=64\quad \frac{n}{2}=32\quad \text{Median class: }29 \leq x < 39$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🔴 Challenging ::::: {#exr-grp-4} ### Modal, Median & Mean (Grouped) Find the modal class, median class, and estimated mean. ```{=html}
\[{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lcc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Midpoint} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 15 \leq x < 24 & 19.5 & 4 \\[4pt] \hline 24 \leq x < 33 & 28.5 & 20 \\[4pt] \hline 33 \leq x < 42 & 37.5 & 11 \\[4pt] \hline 42 \leq x < 51 & 46.5 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}\]
``` a) $$\text{{Write down the modal class.}}$$ b) $$\text{{Identify the median class.}}$$ c) $$\text{{Estimate the mean.}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Modal class: }24 \leq x < 33$$ $$\quad \text{Highest frequency = }20\text{ → modal class: }24 \leq x < 33$$ **b)** $$\text{Median class: }33 \leq x < 42$$ $$\quad n=50\quad \frac{n}{2}=25\quad \text{Build CF to find class containing position }25\quad \text{Median class: }33 \leq x < 42$$ **c)** $$\bar{x} \approx 35.2$$ $$\quad \bar{x}=\dfrac{\sum fx}{\sum f}=\dfrac{19.5\times4+28.5\times20+37.5\times11+46.5\times15}{50}=\dfrac{1758}{50}\approx35.2$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Midpoints Cumulative frequency Reading grouped table
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-grp-5} ### Combined Mean (Two Groups) Find the combined mean of the two groups. a) $$n_1=50,\ \bar{x}_1=17.8\\[4pt]n_2=50,\ \bar{x}_2=10.1$$ b) $$n_1=120,\ \bar{x}_1=23.1\\[4pt]n_2=50,\ \bar{x}_2=13.7$$ c) $$n_1=80,\ \bar{x}_1=23.3\\[4pt]n_2=20,\ \bar{x}_2=15.5$$ d) $$n_1=50,\ \bar{x}_1=23.9\\[4pt]n_2=50,\ \bar{x}_2=21.7$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{Combined mean} = 13.95$$ $$\quad \frac{50\times17.8+50\times10.1}{50+50}=\frac{890.0+505.0}{100}=\frac{1395.0}{100}=13.95$$ **b)** $$\text{Combined mean} = 20.34$$ $$\quad \frac{120\times23.1+50\times13.7}{120+50}=\frac{2772.0+685.0}{170}=\frac{3457.0}{170}=20.34$$ **c)** $$\text{Combined mean} = 21.74$$ $$\quad \frac{80\times23.3+20\times15.5}{80+20}=\frac{1864.0+310.0}{100}=\frac{2174.0}{100}=21.74$$ **d)** $$\text{Combined mean} = 22.8$$ $$\quad \frac{50\times23.9+50\times21.7}{50+50}=\frac{1195.0+1085.0}{100}=\frac{2280.0}{100}=22.8$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Weighted mean Total from mean
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-grp-6} ### Find Missing Frequencies (Grouped) Find the values of a and b. a) $$n = 100,\quad \bar{x} \approx 43.5\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 20 \leq x < 30 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 30 \leq x < 40 & 25 \\[4pt] \hline 40 \leq x < 50 & 35 \\[4pt] \hline 50 \leq x < 60 & a \\[4pt] \hline 60 \leq x < 70 & b \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ b) $$n = 100,\quad \bar{x} \approx 43.5\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 20 \leq x < 30 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 30 \leq x < 40 & 25 \\[4pt] \hline 40 \leq x < 50 & 35 \\[4pt] \hline 50 \leq x < 60 & a \\[4pt] \hline 60 \leq x < 70 & b \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ c) $$n = 100,\quad \bar{x} \approx 43.5\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 20 \leq x < 30 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 30 \leq x < 40 & 25 \\[4pt] \hline 40 \leq x < 50 & 35 \\[4pt] \hline 50 \leq x < 60 & a \\[4pt] \hline 60 \leq x < 70 & b \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ d) $$n = 150,\quad \bar{x} \approx 48\\[6pt]{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & \text{Frequency} \\[4pt] \hline 20 \leq x < 30 & 13 \\[4pt] \hline 30 \leq x < 40 & 15 \\[4pt] \hline 40 \leq x < 50 & 40 \\[4pt] \hline 50 \leq x < 60 & a \\[4pt] \hline 60 \leq x < 70 & b \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$a = 10,\ b = 15$$ $$\quad \text{Step 1: }a+b=100-75=25\\[4pt]\text{Step 2: }\Sigma fx=43.5\times100=4350\\[4pt]\text{Known }\Sigma fx=2825\quad\Rightarrow 55a+65b=1525\\[4pt]\text{Step 3: Solve simultaneously: }a=10,\ b=15$$ **b)** $$a = 10,\ b = 15$$ $$\quad \text{Step 1: }a+b=100-75=25\\[4pt]\text{Step 2: }\Sigma fx=43.5\times100=4350\\[4pt]\text{Known }\Sigma fx=2825\quad\Rightarrow 55a+65b=1525\\[4pt]\text{Step 3: Solve simultaneously: }a=10,\ b=15$$ **c)** $$a = 10,\ b = 15$$ $$\quad \text{Step 1: }a+b=100-75=25\\[4pt]\text{Step 2: }\Sigma fx=43.5\times100=4350\\[4pt]\text{Known }\Sigma fx=2825\quad\Rightarrow 55a+65b=1525\\[4pt]\text{Step 3: Solve simultaneously: }a=10,\ b=15$$ **d)** $$a = 78,\ b = 4$$ $$\quad \text{Step 1: }a+b=150-68=82\\[4pt]\text{Step 2: }\Sigma fx=48\times150=7200\\[4pt]\text{Known }\Sigma fx=2650\quad\Rightarrow 55a+65b=4550\\[4pt]\text{Step 3: Solve simultaneously: }a=78,\ b=4$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Simultaneous equations Mean formula
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: :::::::::: --- ## 📈 Part D — Cumulative Frequency {#sec-cumfreq} ::: {.callout-note icon=false} ## 🔑 Key Points | Measure | Position on CF axis | |---------|-------------------| | Median ($Q_2$) | $\frac{n}{2}$ | | Lower quartile ($Q_1$) | $\frac{n}{4}$ | | Upper quartile ($Q_3$) | $\frac{3n}{4}$ | $\text{IQR} = Q_3 - Q_1$ ::: :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-cf-1} ### Build Cumulative Frequency Table Complete the cumulative frequency table. a) $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & f \\[4pt] \hline 28 \leq x < 37 & 5 \\[4pt] \hline 37 \leq x < 46 & 11 \\[4pt] \hline 46 \leq x < 55 & 13 \\[4pt] \hline 55 \leq x < 64 & 25 \\[4pt] \hline 64 \leq x < 73 & 24 \\[4pt] \hline 73 \leq x < 82 & 25 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ```{=html}
``` ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\begin{array}{lc} \hline x & \text{Cum. Freq} \\[4pt] \hline x < 37 & 5 \\[4pt] \hline x < 46 & 16 \\[4pt] \hline x < 55 & 29 \\[4pt] \hline x < 64 & 54 \\[4pt] \hline x < 73 & 78 \\[4pt] \hline x < 82 & 103 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}$$ $$\quad \text{Running totals: }5, 16, 29, 54, 78, 103$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

Freq} \\[4pt] \hline x < 39 & 12 \\[4pt] \hline x < 54 & 30 \\[4pt] \hline x < 69 & 34 \\[4pt] \hline x < 84 & 54 \\[4pt] \hline x < 99 & 62 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}\\[8pt]\text{(a) Items} \leq 54\text{?}\\[6pt]\text{(b) Items} > 54\text{?}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\text{(a) }30\quad\text{(b) }32$$ $$\quad \text{(a) CF at }54=30\quad\text{(b) }62-30=32$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

\[{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & f \\[4pt] \hline 16 \leq x < 35 & 18 \\[4pt] \hline 35 \leq x < 54 & 5 \\[4pt] \hline 54 \leq x < 73 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline 73 \leq x < 92 & 23 \\[4pt] \hline 92 \leq x < 111 & 14 \\[4pt] \hline 111 \leq x < 130 & 7 \\[4pt] \hline 130 \leq x < 149 & 12 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}\]

``` ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $${\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline x & \text{Cum. Freq} \\[4pt] \hline x < 35 & 18 \\[4pt] \hline x < 54 & 23 \\[4pt] \hline x < 73 & 32 \\[4pt] \hline x < 92 & 55 \\[4pt] \hline x < 111 & 69 \\[4pt] \hline x < 130 & 76 \\[4pt] \hline x < 149 & 88 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ $$\quad \text{Running totals: }18, 23, 32, 55, 69, 76, 88$$ **b)** $$\text{Median} \approx 82.9$$ $$\quad \text{Read at }\frac{n}{2}=\frac{88}{2}=44\text{ on CF axis}\quad \text{Median}\approx82.9$$ **c)** $$\text{IQR} = 56.7$$ $$\quad Q_1\approx50.2\text{ (read at }22.0\text{)}\quad Q_3\approx106.9\text{ (read at }66.0\text{)}\quad \text{IQR}=106.9-50.2=56.7$$ **d)** $$\approx 31\text{ items}$$ $$\quad \text{Read CF at }95\approx57\quad \text{Above: }88-57=31$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

Freq} \\[4pt] \hline x < 21 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline x < 28 & A \\[4pt] \hline x < 35 & 42 \\[4pt] \hline x < 42 & B \\[4pt] \hline x < 49 & 73 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$A = 29,\ B = 55$$ $$\quad A = 9+20=29\quad B = 42+13=55$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-cf-6} ### Cumulative Frequency — Full Analysis Use the frequency table and cumulative frequency graph to answer the questions. ```{=html}
\[{\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.6}\begin{array}{lc} \hline \text{Class} & f \\[4pt] \hline 28 \leq x < 39 & 19 \\[4pt] \hline 39 \leq x < 50 & 17 \\[4pt] \hline 50 \leq x < 61 & 20 \\[4pt] \hline 61 \leq x < 72 & 6 \\[4pt] \hline 72 \leq x < 83 & 9 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}}\]
``` a) $$\text{Complete the cumulative frequency table.}$$ b) $\text{Estimate the median from the graph.}$ c) $\text{Estimate }Q_1\text{ and }Q_3.$ d) $\text{Calculate the IQR.}$ e) $\text{Estimate how many items are above }57.$ ```{=html}
``` ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$\begin{array}{lc} \hline x & \text{CF} \\[4pt] \hline x < 39 & 19 \\[4pt] \hline x < 50 & 36 \\[4pt] \hline x < 61 & 56 \\[4pt] \hline x < 72 & 62 \\[4pt] \hline x < 83 & 71 \\[4pt] \hline \end{array}$$ $$\quad \text{Running totals: }19, 36, 56, 62, 71$$ **b)** $\text{Median} \approx 49.7$ $$\quad \text{Read at }\frac{n}{2}=\frac{71}{2}=35\quad \Rightarrow \text{Median}\approx49.7$$ **c)** $Q_1 \approx 38.3,\quad Q_3 \approx 59.5$ $$\quad Q_1\text{: read at }\frac{n}{4}=17.8\Rightarrow Q_1\approx38.3\\[4pt]Q_3\text{: read at }\frac{3n}{4}=53.2\Rightarrow Q_3\approx59.5$$ **d)** $\text{IQR} = 21.2$ $$\quad \text{IQR} = Q_3 - Q_1 = 59.5 - 38.3 = 21.2$$ **e)** $\approx 22\text{ items}$ $$\quad \text{Read CF at }57\approx49\quad 71-49=22$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: :::::::::: --- ## 🎯 Part E — Box Plots and IQR {#sec-boxplot} ::: {.callout-note icon=false} ## 🔑 Key Points $$\text{IQR} = Q_3 - Q_1$$ **Outlier rule:** A value is an outlier if it is $> Q_3 + 1.5 \times \text{IQR}$ or $< Q_1 - 1.5 \times \text{IQR}$ ::: :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-bp-1} ### IQR from Raw Data Find the interquartile range. a) $4,\ 9,\ 9,\ 10,\ 12,\ 12,\ 13,\ 17,\ 19,\ 22,\ 23,\ 24,\ 26,\ 29,\ 30$ ```{=html}
``` ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $\text{IQR} = 14$ $$\quad Q_1=10,\ Q_3=24\quad \text{IQR}=24-10=14$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Quartile calculation Subtraction

::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-bp-4} ### Find Unknown Values from Box Plot Conditions Find the unknown values using the given conditions. a) $$h,\ 5,\ 5,\ 7,\ j,\ 15,\ k,\ k\\[6pt]\text{Median}=8,\ \text{Mode}=20,\ \text{Range}=17$$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$h=3,\ j=9,\ k=20$$ $$\quad k=20\text{ (mode, appears twice)}\quad h=k-17=3\text{ (range)}\quad j=2\times8-7=9\text{ (median)}$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Simultaneous conditions Median rule Mode rule Range rule
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-bp-5} ### Read a Box Plot Use the box plot to answer the questions. ```{=html}
``` a) $\text{Write down the minimum value.}$ b) $\text{Write down }Q_1.$ c) $\text{Write down the median.}$ d) $\text{Write down }Q_3.$ e) $\text{Calculate the IQR.}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $\text{Min} = 15$ $$\quad \text{Read the left whisker end: }15$$ **b)** $Q_1 = 21$ $$\quad \text{Read the left edge of the box: }21$$ **c)** $\text{Median} = 27$ $$\quad \text{Read the line inside the box: }27$$ **d)** $Q_3 = 35$ $$\quad \text{Read the right edge of the box: }35$$ **e)** $\text{IQR} = 14$ $$\quad \text{IQR} = Q_3 - Q_1 = 35 - 21 = 14$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reading box plot Quartile identification Iqr calculation
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🔴 Challenging ::::: {#exr-bp-3} ### Identify Outliers (1.5 × IQR Rule) Identify outliers using the 1.5 × IQR rule and draw a box plot. a) $$14,\ 22,\ 24,\ 30,\ 31,\ 37,\ 48,\ 48,\ 65,\ 67,\ 107$$ ```{=html}
``` ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution **a)** $$Q_1=24,\ Q_3=65,\ \text{IQR}=41\\\text{Upper fence}=126.5\\107>126.5\Rightarrow\text{outlier}$$ $$\quad \text{Step 1: }Q_1=24,\ Q_3=65\\[4pt]\text{Step 2: IQR}=Q_3-Q_1=65-24=41\\[4pt]\text{Step 3: Upper fence}=Q_3+1.5\times\text{IQR}=65+1.5\times41=126.5\\[4pt]\text{Step 4: }107>126.5\therefore\text{ outlier }\checkmark$$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
